concept: Exclude / Keep or Leave Outside an Activity or Group
description: To keep or leave a person or thing outside a stated group, activity, place, resource, or process. Exclusion may be deliberate or produced by an arrangement whose effect was ignored. The opening shared with 'pharune' (include) keeps both words about the same boundary; their endings tell which way the boundary moves.
sound_symbolism: Breathy 'pha' reaches the shared edge also heard in 'pharune' (include). Rounded 'ro' turns away, and closed 'mu' leaves the excluded party outside the active space.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'shia mia pharomu.' (They exclude me.) A prepositional phrase can name the group, place, activity, or process. The event-noun rule supplies exclusion, while a passive clause can foreground the excluded party.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A commons fails at its own edge when the people most affected cannot enter, contribute, or use what is held in common.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The verb makes exclusion reportable even when nobody announced it and the closed door was a rule, format, or deadline.

tags.activity: preventing or omitting participation in a stated action
tags.community: keeping someone outside a group or shared process
tags.spatial: denying entry to a place or route
